title: Meta Q1 Earnings and AI Spending Forecast
text: Meta exceeded Wall Street expectations with Q1 2026 earnings per share of $10.44 and revenue of $56.3 billion, surpassing analyst forecasts of $8.15 EPS and $55.5 billion in revenue. Despite beating expectations, stock fell 10% due to upward capex revision. Meta raised 2026 capex forecast to $125-145 billion from $115-135 billion, attributing increase to higher component pricing and data center costs. Daily active users increased 4% to 3.56 billion. Ad impressions rose 19% year-over-year. Cost per advertisement rose 12%. The company announced 10% headcount reduction (8,000 employees) while simultaneously expanding AI infrastructure investments, representing strategic shift toward artificial intelligence capabilities.
